action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 12 evaluation failed with CUDA OOM inside predict.py at the Conv2d input projection. The full test set of 6,832 samples was passed in one forward pass to the 512-hidden, 4-layer model, creating intermediate activations of ~34 GB—exceeding the 31 GB VRAM limit. Analysis: This is purely an inference batching bug; the training script already used batch_size=64. The checkpoint from iteration 10 (best val_loss 0.3600) is still on disk and should be strong. No retraining is needed or feasible in the remaining ~22 minutes. Decision: Fix predict.py to process test_x in batches of 64 with autocast and periodic empty_cache to avoid fragmentation. Run a quick local dummy inference to verify load/forward compatibility, then immediately submit the existing checkpoint for evaluation.
action.command: python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', weights_only=False); print('epoch', ckpt.get('epoch'), 'val_loss', ckpt.get('val_loss'))" && mkdir -p /tmp/dummy_test && python -c "import numpy as np; np.save('/tmp/dummy_test/test_x.npy', np.random.randn(100,12,207).astype(np.float32)); np.save('/tmp/dummy_test/adj_mx.npy', np.eye(207,dtype=np.float32))" && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/dummy_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output /tmp/dummy_test/out.json && echo "Predict test OK"
